title: taxus baccata var. stricta
intro: Taxus baccata var. stricta, also known as the strict yew, is a variety of the yew plant species Taxus baccata. It is a slow-growing evergreen tree native to Europe and Western Asia, known for its distinctive red berries and dark green leaves. The strict yew has been used in herbal medicine for centuries, primarily for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to have several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and improving cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, or salves, although its use should be approached with caution due to the presence of toxic compounds.
